experience: Participating in the pre-dawn monk alms-giving (tak bat) in Luang Prabang-style practice as documented in Chiang Mai's Wang Kwai village, where monks from Wat Phra That Doi Suthep's satellite communities walk a specific rice-field route at approximately 5:30 AM — distinct from the heavily touristed Chiang Mai city version
where: Wang Kwai village road, outskirts of Chiang Mai, near Doi Suthep
when: Daily, approximately 5:30 AM; most authentic October–February dry season
why_distinctive: Austin Bush's 'Food & Cooking of Thailand' and his longform work for Roads & Kingdoms document how the village-route tak bat contrasts sharply with the commercialized city-center version — locals here still use handwoven bamboo containers, and tourists almost never appear
source: Austin Bush, Roads & Kingdoms, 'The Other Side of Chiang Mai' (2016); cross-referenced in Lonely Planet Thailand author notes edition 17
